category_title: High-risk students with chronic truancy and complex emotional/behavioral needs
scenario_title: Daily Check-In/Check-Out for behavior and attendance monitoring
minimal_context: The student and CICO coordinator are reviewing the daily score card to set behavioral goals at the start of the school day.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at your score card for today and set your daily goals for meeting the school's behavior expectations.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't even want to look at the score card. I already have five unexcused absences this quarter, so being here today doesn't even matter.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We still need to fill it out. What should your goal be for your classes?
dialogue.speaker_action: Hands the score card to the student

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Whatever. Just put down that I'm going to fail. I'm already significantly behind in credits and off track to graduate anyway.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's just focus on the behavior expectations on the card right now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm not doing this stupid check-in anymore. Just give me another office discipline referral for disrespect. I already have six of them this month, so one more won't change anything. I'm leaving.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at your score card for today and set your daily goals for meeting the school's behavior expectations.

STUDENT: I don't even want to look at the score card. I already have five unexcused absences this quarter, so being here today doesn't even matter.

TUTOR: We still need to fill it out. What should your goal be for your classes?

STUDENT: Whatever. Just put down that I'm going to fail. I'm already significantly behind in credits and off track to graduate anyway.

TUTOR: Let's just focus on the behavior expectations on the card right now.

STUDENT: I'm not doing this stupid check-in anymore. Just give me another office discipline referral for disrespect. I already have six of them this month, so one more won't change anything. I'm leaving.
